diagnosis: Enhancer stage densifies both RNA and ATAC matrices for the per-peak Pearson loop.
200k cells × 30k peaks float32 ATAC dense = 24 GB. Plus 200k × 8k RNA = 6.4 GB.
Combined ~30 GB densification on a 32 GB laptop triggers heavy paging.
Single-threaded Python loop over peaks made stall observable: process at 50% CPU, no progress for ~50 min.
100k×50k bench succeeded because exact peak RSS reporting on macOS undercounts swapped pages — the actual working set there was likely also far above ru_maxrss.
Real fix: rewrite enhancer correlation to operate on the sparse ATAC matrix without densifying. v0.4 candidate.
next_step: Rewrite link_peaks_to_genes to keep ATAC sparse during the correlation loop (sparse Pearson trick: precompute per-column means and norms, do sparse dot-products column-wise).
